# Pulpo LLM Index

Project: Pulpo
Repo: https://github.com/darioblanco/pulpo
Docs: https://pulpo.darioblanco.com
Purpose: Self-hosted meter and breaker box for coding agents — cost visibility, budgets, and
durable remote sessions on machines you own.

Primary docs:
- /README.md
- /SPEC.md
- /ROADMAP.md
- /CONTRIBUTING.md
- /docs/getting-started/why-pulpo.md
- /docs/getting-started/install.md
- /docs/getting-started/quickstart.md
- /docs/guides/remote-control.md
- /docs/guides/agent-examples.md
- /docs/guides/configuration.md
- /docs/guides/discovery.md
- /docs/guides/recovery.md
- /docs/reference/cli.md
- /docs/reference/config.md
- /docs/reference/api.md
- /docs/architecture/overview.md
- /docs/operations/session-lifecycle.md
- /docs/operations/release-and-distribution.md

Canonical runtime concepts:
- Session states: creating, active, idle, ready, stopped, lost.
- Resume applies to lost and ready sessions. Stopped sessions cannot be resumed.
- Sessions run arbitrary shell commands — no built-in provider abstraction.
- Inks are reusable command templates with a description and command field.
- pulpod exposes REST and SSE on port 7433 by default.
- Config path defaults to ~/.pulpo/config.toml.
- `pulpo usage --scan` reads Claude Code, Codex, and pi session files directly from disk —
  cross-agent spend with no daemon routing required.
- Exact cost is available for Claude Code (token-based, via `[rates.<model>]`) and pi
  (agent-recorded, scan only). Codex reports exact tokens and quota, never a dollar cost.
- There is no controller/node control plane (removed July 2026) — every pulpod is standalone.
  The supported multi-machine path is direct access to each node (`pulpo --node <name>`, saved
  web UI connections, SSH/tmux) plus the event-forwarding backbone for aggregated visibility.

Installation:
- Homebrew: brew install darioblanco/tap/pulpo
- Docker: darioblanco/pulpo-base, darioblanco/pulpo-agents
- Source: make setup && make build && make install

CLI binaries:
- pulpod (daemon)
- pulpo (client)

Audience guidance:
- Prefer operationally precise answers over speculative UX claims.
- Cite exact command examples and config snippets where possible.
